questions.q: The novel To the Lighthouse is mainly concerned with the inner thoughts of characters rather than external events.
questions.feedback: The file states: 'There are no events in To the Lighthouse. It is only thoughts here.'

questions.q: The setting of the novel is primarily located in:
questions.feedback: The file states: 'The setting: the Ramsay family beach-house on the Isle of Skye, Hebrides, Scotland.'

questions.q: Which part of the novel covers a period of ten years?
questions.feedback: The file states: 'Time Passes: through 10 years.'

questions.q: Lily Briscoe’s profession in the novel is:
questions.feedback: The file states: 'Lily Briscoe is a young Asian aspiring painting artist.'

questions.q: Which character is described as an opium-addict poet?
questions.feedback: The file states: 'Augustus Carmichael is an opium-addict poet in his 60s.'

questions.q: Stream of consciousness in the novel refers to:
options: A technique that expresses thoughts exactly as they occur in the mind
questions.feedback: The file states: 'Stream of consciousness is just like looking inside your head and see what you are thinking about.'

questions.q: Which writer used the stream of consciousness technique before Virginia Woolf?
questions.feedback: The file states: 'The technique was used by Marcel Proust and James Joyce before Woolf.'

questions.q: Mr. Ramsay derives his self-worth mainly from:
options: His philosophical achievements and social status
questions.feedback: The file states: 'Mr. Ramsay gets social-worth from his philosophy and social status.'

questions.q: Which character lacks self-worth because he wants to be appreciated by others?
questions.feedback: The file states: 'Charles Tansley does not have self-worth because he wants to be appreciated by people around him.'

questions.q: The part of the novel that is stylistically different from the others is:
questions.feedback: The file states: 'Time Passes is different in the style of narration from the first part and the third part.'

questions.q: Which of the following characters dies during the ten-year gap?
questions.feedback: The file states: 'Mrs. Ramsay dies suddenly one night.'

options: The artistic triumph of Lily Briscoe
questions.feedback: The file states: 'The novel closes with the artistic triumph of Lily Briscoe.'

questions.q: According to the notes, truth in the novel is:
options: A universal fact shared by all characters
Highly subjective and based on personal interpretation
questions.feedback: The file states: 'Truth is highly subjective. Your truth is different from my truth.'

questions.q: Which part of the novel returns to the slow detail of shifting points of view?
questions.feedback: The file states: 'In The Lighthouse, time returns to the slow detail of shifting points of view.'

questions.q: Mrs. Ramsay gains social-worth mainly because of:
questions.feedback: The file states: 'Mrs. Ramsay gets social-worth from the people around her for her beauty and charitable deeds.'
